llm_result.summary: The case reveals rapid, context-driven irritability (C01) with clear triggers (C02), followed by post-episode dysregulation (C03) and self-directed distress (C04), alongside a values-behavior gap (C05) and maladaptive coping (C06) that strain the couple (C07). Modifiable levers span emotion regulation, trigger management, interpersonal communication, post-conflict recovery, self-compassion, sleep and nutrition, mindfulness, and social support. The plan targets proximal predictors to reduce reactivity, shorten recovery after conflicts, and strengthen repair in the relationship, documented by simple, scalable measures over a short-term horizon (weeks). Each lever is a practical, changeable predictor designed for quick implementation and monitoring, with attention to safety where distress escalates.

solutions.predictor_variable: Emotion Regulation Skill Capacity (ER) – cognitive reappraisal, impulse control, urge surfing
solutions.solution_candidate: Structured emotion-regulation training using CBT/DBT-informed modules and brief guided practice (e.g., 15–20 minutes daily) plus real-time urge surfing during triggering moments.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Improves top-down control over limbic arousal, reducing impulsive snapping (C01) and stabilizing affect after conflicts (C03); supports kinder behavior in line with intention (C05) and lowers self-directed distress (C04).
solutions.measurement: Daily anger outbursts (count), self-rated urge to snap (0–10), brief emotion-regulation skill usage (checklists) and pre/post DERS-18 scores.
solutions.safety_notes: No safety concerns identified; monitor for suicidality or worsening distress.
solutions.mapping_query: Assess whether improving emotion regulation capacity reduces irritability (C01), post-conflict dysregulation (C03), distress (C04), and the gap between values and behavior (C05). Include pre/post ER measures and anger frequency.

solutions.solution_candidate: Modify or schedule household tasks and communication norms to reduce cue-driven snapping (e.g., dishwashing rotation, explicit tone-communication protocols, predictable routines).
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Diminishes reliable provocation cues, lowering the likelihood and intensity of reactive outbursts and relational strain.
solutions.measurement: Trigger diary; frequency of snapping episodes; time-to-cool-down after triggering cue.
solutions.safety_notes: None specific; ensure collaborative agreement on changes to avoid teammate perceptions of control loss.
solutions.mapping_query: Map environmental changes to reductions in irritability (C01), trigger sensitivity (C02), and relational impact (C07); track cue-related events.

solutions.solution_candidate: Teach and practice repair attempts, I-statements, and escalation-reduction scripts in brief sessions plus in-the-moment coaching during conversations.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Enhances dyadic repair, reduces escalation, and narrows the gap between intention and action (C05) by providing concrete tools for constructive discourse.
solutions.measurement: Number of successful repair attempts; self-reported communication efficacy; partner-rated conversation quality.
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor for avoidance of conflict avoidance; encourage continued two-way dialogue.
solutions.mapping_query: Evaluate impact of structured communication on relationship strain (C07) and alignment with kind intent (C05) through repair occurrence metrics.

solutions.predictor_variable: Post-Conflict Recovery Plan (Cooling-off and Repair Scheduling)
solutions.solution_candidate: Pre-agreed cooling-off period after conflicts (e.g., 10–15 minutes) followed by a guided repair conversation; coached by a clinician or structured self-guide.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Prevents prolonged dysregulation, supports rapid re-regulation after episodes (C03), reduces self-blame (C04) and reliance on withdrawal coping (C06).
solutions.measurement: Time to first repair after conflict; frequency of withdrawal episodes; post-conflict distress scale.
solutions.safety_notes: If distress escalates or self-harm risk emerges, seek professional guidance immediately.
solutions.mapping_query: Assess effect of structured cooling-off and repair sequencing on post-episode dysregulation (C03) and withdrawal coping (C06).

solutions.solution_candidate: Exercises to cultivate self-compassion and clarify values, reducing self-judgment after episodes and bridging values-behavior gaps.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reduces harsh self-criticism driving distress (C04) and supports enactment of kind intentions (C05) by aligning actions with core values; improves tolerance to frustration (C01).
solutions.measurement: Self-compassion scale scores; diary entries linking values to actions; perceived alignment rating.
solutions.safety_notes: None specific; ensure support for self-acceptance and non-judgmental reflection.
solutions.mapping_query: Link self-compassion and values alignment to reductions in distress (C04) and behavior congruence (C05), with notes on irritation control (C01).

solutions.solution_candidate: Establish stable sleep-wake times, optimize sleep environment, limit caffeine, and align circadian cues to improve emotional regulation.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Better sleep enhances prefrontal control and emotional stability, reducing irritability (C01) and dysregulated episodes (C03); improves mood/self-perception (C04).
solutions.measurement: Sleep diary metrics (duration, consistency), PSQI score, next-morning mood scale.
solutions.mapping_query: Evaluate sleep quality and regularity as a predictor of irritability and post-conflict regulation (C01, C03, C04).

solutions.solution_candidate: Assess and optimize macronutrient/nutrient intake and hydration to support neurotransmitter function and energy stability.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Adequate nutrition supports neural function and energy, reducing irritability (C01) and maladaptive withdrawal coping (C06).
solutions.measurement: Dietary logs; intake adequacy of key nutrients; mood/irritability ratings.
solutions.mapping_query: Track nutrient adequacy and glycemic stability to predict changes in irritability (C01) and withdrawal coping (C06).

solutions.solution_candidate: Daily mindfulness practices (breath-focused, body scan) with brief check-ins after triggering events to observe thoughts without reacting.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Nonjudgmental awareness decreases reactivity, supports rapid affect regulation, and reduces self-distress following episodes.
solutions.measurement: State mindfulness ratings; daily rumination; frequency/severity of anger after triggers.
solutions.mapping_query: Examine how mindfulness practice relates to reductions in irritability (C01), post-conflict dysregulation (C03), and distress (C04).

solutions.solution_candidate: Structured social support: regular check-ins with partner or a trusted friend, optimistic framing, and collaborative problem-solving.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Social buffering reduces perceived stress, promotes repair, and lowers distress after conflicts.
solutions.measurement: Perceived social support scale; frequency/quality of supportive interactions; partner-reported warmth/trust.
solutions.safety_notes: Encourage positive, non-pressured support; avoid enabling avoidance of responsibility.
solutions.mapping_query: Assess whether enhanced social support and partner involvement buffer irritability (C01) and distress (C04) via supportive interactions and repair opportunities.
